text: Planning a short trip does not have to be complicated.
Start by choosing a nearby destination that you can reach in two or three hours.
You do not need a passport or an expensive flight — sometimes the best places are close to home.
Before you go, check a map and learn how to navigate the area on foot or by bus.
If the landscape includes steep hills, pack comfortable shoes.
Read a few reviews to find an outstanding place to eat, and look for authentic local dishes rather than tourist restaurants.
The biggest obstacle most travellers face is trying to do too much.
A weekend is not long enough to overcome exhaustion from a packed schedule.
Pick two or three things you truly want to see, and leave the rest for another visit.
The goal is to return home feeling rested, not more tired than when you left.
